title: MIND: Multi-Agent AI Co-Scientist Framework Automates Hypothesis-to-Experiment Loop
summary: MIND introduces an LLM-driven multi-agent framework for automated scientific research that goes beyond text-based reasoning to include automated experimental verification — addressing a key gap in prior AI-for-science systems that stopped at hypothesis generation. The framework orchestrates specialized agents for hypothesis formation, experimental design, and result analysis in material science research. Demonstrates a pattern (agent-driven hypothesis → automated experiment → feedback loop) applicable beyond materials science to any domain with automatable experiments.

title: SHAPR: Operationalizing Human-AI Research Practice — Structured Knowledge Generation Framework with Traceable Decision Cycles
summary: Chan presents the operational implementation of SHAPR (Solo Human-Centred and AI-Assisted Practice), a framework for structuring research software development that integrates human decision-making with AI assistance through iterative Explore-Build-Validate cycles. The framework produces traceable, structured knowledge artifacts at each stage, addressing the reproducibility gap in AI-assisted research workflows where decisions are typically undocumented.

title: NVIDIA VibeTensor: AI-Generated Deep Learning Runtime
summary: Complete PyTorch-style runtime built entirely by coding agents
